comments.body: Done (commit pending). docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md (front-matter+closed key set+override mechanism+write-back detection; contract conventions prompt inputs/outputs vs op input_schema/output_schema → named registry schemas; derived index+CI drift gate; 3-state OK/UNKNOWN/ERROR+abstain+runtime-against-consumer; execution_mode). docs/adr/0001-execution-mode-fixed-on-prompt.md (decision + rejected swappable-strategy alternative). CLAUDE.md points to editor doc + authoring-v2 doc + adr/.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The per-child plan-review flagged (T7, on nearly every child) that the new author-facing conventions have
no documentation artifact. Handle it ONCE here rather than per-child, so the conventions are discoverable.

## What
Author the workflow-authoring-v2 docs + ADRs covering: the prompt FRONT-MATTER format + closed key set;
the `execution_mode` divergence (an ADR — why fixed-on-prompt vs DSPy/BAML/pydantic-ai swappable strategy);
the step CONTRACT conventions (`inputs`/`outputs` ↔ `input_schema`/`output_schema` ↔ named registry schema);
the `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` OVERRIDE mechanism + write-back location detection; the derived-index LOCKFILE +
CI drift gate; and the 3-state VALIDATION semantics (OK/UNKNOWN/ERROR + abstain rules + runtime-against-consumer).

## Scope
In: docs/ + ADR(s) + CLAUDE.md pointers for the above. Out: the implementations themselves (their own stories).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A docs page documents the prompt front-matter format + closed key set + the override mechanism.
- [ ] An ADR records the `execution_mode`-fixed-on-prompt decision and the rejected swappable-strategy alternative.
- [ ] The contract conventions (prompt `inputs`/`outputs` vs op `input_schema`/`output_schema`, both → registry names) are documented.
- [ ] The derived-index lockfile + CI drift gate, and the 3-state validation semantics, are documented.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md / the agent guide points to the above so authors can find them.
compiled_state.title: Docs + ADRs for workflow authoring v2 (front-matter, execution_mode, contracts, validation)
